docs.title: Welcome to OmniPackage
docs.text:
Documentation in progress

This documentation is still being written. In the meantime, check out Getting started to install the CLI and build your first package.

For the source code and examples, visit the project on GitHub.

docs.title: Supported distros
docs.text:
OmniPackage can build DEB and RPM packages for the distributions below. Each has a distro ID used in config.yml (builds[].distro) and on the command line (--distros <id>).

The list is fetched live from distros.yml in the OmniPackage repo, so it always reflects what is currently supported.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: All distros currently target x86_64. ARM/ARM64 is not yet supported.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Real working projects live in the examples repo. Each has a ready-to-use .omnipackage/config.yml and a README with specifics.

docs.title: Language / build-system samples
docs.text:
• C + Makefile — the walkthrough used in Getting started
  • Rust / Cargo
  • Go
  • Python
  • Ruby
  • Crystal
  • Tauri (desktop app)
  • Electron (desktop app)

docs.title: Topic samples
docs.text:
• Using secrets — passing ${...} values from .env into builds
  • Custom build scripts — before_build_script hook

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: OmniPackage turns a source project into signed DEB and RPM repositories. This page walks through installing the CLI and building the bundled C example end-to-end.

docs.title: Install the CLI
docs.text:
There are two ways to install it:

  1. OmniPackage repositories
  2. Source code — requires Rust 1.85+ (2024 edition)

Test your installation: 

omnipackage --version

You should see something like omnipackage 0.1.4.

docs.title: Build the example project
docs.text:
Clone the examples repo and enter the C/Makefile sample:

git clone https://github.com/omnipackage/examples.git && cd examples/c_makefile

Generate a GPG key. This key is required to sign the packages. You can use your existing key.

echo "GPG_KEY=$(omnipackage gpg generate --name 'Your Name' --email 'you@example.com' --format base64)" >> .env

The key is ASCII-armored and then base64-encoded so it can live in an env variable without newline escaping.

By default omnipackage expects a .env file in the root of the project, but it can be overridden by the --env-file option. .env now holds your private signing key — keep it out of version control (the examples repo already gitignores it).

Run the release command (. is the project directory containing .omnipackage/config.yml):

omnipackage release .

You'll see the build log for each distro listed in .omnipackage/config.yml.

This will create local repositories at /tmp/omnipackage-repos. Open /tmp/omnipackage-repos/install.html in a browser — it's a generated landing page with copy-paste instructions for adding the repo on each distro. Path /tmp/omnipackage-repos is defined in .omnipackage/config.yml under repositories, and by default the first entry there is used by the release command.

Note that /tmp is cleared on reboot on many Linux systems; pick a persistent path in config.yml if you want the local repo to survive.

docs.title: Switch to S3 for production
docs.text:
Local repositories are fine for testing, but for production you probably want S3. An example S3 block is already provided in .omnipackage/config.yml. Pick it by name with --repository:

omnipackage release . --repository "Example bucket"

docs.title: How secrets flow
docs.text: All secrets must be declared in config.yml and passed from env via ${...} syntax. config.yml stays the only source of truth; .env (or any other env file, or the actual environment) holds the values. There are no hidden env settings beyond what's declared in config.

docs.title: CLI reference
docs.text: OmniPackage ships a single binary, omnipackage, with several subcommands.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text: Command Purpose build Build packages inside containers, don't publish publish Upload already-built packages to a repository release build followed by publish info Query project metadata (distros, install-page URL) gpg Generate and convert signing keys portal Open an interactive shell in a distro's build container prime Pre-populate the image cache

docs.title: Global options
docs.text: Flag Description --container-runtime <docker\|podman> Override autodetection. Also OMNIPACKAGE_CONTAINER_RUNTIME. --env-file <path> Env file to load (default: .env in project root).

docs.title: omnipackage build
docs.text:
Build packages for the distros defined in .omnipackage/config.yml, without publishing.

omnipackage build <project-dir> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --distros <ids> Comma-separated subset of distros to build (default: all defined in config). --build-dir <path> Where intermediate build artefacts go (default: .omnipackage/build). --fail-fast Stop on the first failing distro instead of building the rest. --image-cache <name> Use a configured image cache. --version-extractor <name> Pick a version extractor by name. --container-output Stream container output to stdout during the build. --disable-container-echo Suppress container output entirely. --fail-log-lines <n> On failure, print the last N lines of the build log.

docs.title: omnipackage gpg
docs.text: Generate and convert GPG signing keys. See the Signing packages guide for the broader workflow.

docs.title: gpg generate
docs.text:
Generate a new keypair and print it to stdout.

omnipackage gpg generate --name <name> --email <email> --format <pem|base64>
Flag Description --name Key owner name --email Key owner email --format pem for ASCII armor, base64 for base64-wrapped armor (suitable for env vars)

docs.title: gpg convert
docs.text:
Convert between key formats.

omnipackage gpg convert [--input <file>] --input-format <pem|base64> --output-format <pem|base64>

Reads stdin if --input is omitted. Always writes to stdout.

docs.title: omnipackage info
docs.text:
Query project metadata without building anything. Useful in CI scripts.

omnipackage info <project-dir> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --list-distros Print the distros defined in config.yml (JSON or plain text). --show-install-page-url Print the URL where the install page will be published. --repository <name> Scope the query to a specific repository entry.

docs.title: omnipackage portal
docs.text:
Open an interactive shell inside the build container for a given distro. The working directory, dependencies, and env match a real build — this is the debugging tool when a build fails and you want to poke around.

omnipackage portal <distro-id> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --build-dir <path> Build directory to mount.

docs.title: omnipackage prime
docs.text:
Pre-populate the container image cache by running the setup stage for each distro and snapshotting the result. Later build / release runs start from the cached image and skip setup.

omnipackage prime <project-dir> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --distros <ids> Subset of distros to prime. --build-dir <path> Build directory. --fail-fast Stop on first failing distro. --image-cache <name> Which image cache to populate.

Typical use: run prime once as a scheduled CI job so interactive releases are fast.

docs.title: omnipackage publish
docs.text:
Upload already-built packages to a repository. Assumes omnipackage build ran first.

omnipackage publish <project-dir> [flags]

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Description --distros <ids> Comma-separated subset of distros to publish. --build-dir <path> Where to read built artefacts from. --fail-fast Stop on the first failing distro. --image-cache <name> Image cache to use for repo-metadata generation containers. --repository <name> Pick a repository by name (default: first entry in config). --custom-install-page <path> Path to a custom install.html template. --container-output Stream container output to stdout. --disable-container-echo Suppress container output.

docs.title: omnipackage release
docs.text:
Convenience command that runs build followed by publish.

omnipackage release <project-dir> [flags]

Accepts the union of build and publish flags. See those pages for the full list.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: OmniPackage reads .omnipackage/config.yml from the project root. This section documents every top-level key.

docs.title: Top-level keys
docs.text: Key Required Purpose version_extractors yes How to determine the package version builds yes Per-distro build configuration repositories no Where to publish built packages image_caches no Cached container images to speed up builds secrets no Environment-variable substitution into config.yml

docs.title: Environment substitution
docs.text: Any string value in config.yml can reference an environment variable with ${VAR} syntax. Values come from .env (default) or --env-file <path>. No hidden env settings — only variables referenced in config.yml are consumed.

docs.title: builds
docs.text: Each entry in builds: defines one package build for one target distro. A project that ships to Debian 12 and Fedora 40 has two builds entries (often deduplicated with YAML anchors).

docs.title: Keys per build entry
docs.text: Key Required Description distro yes Distro ID — see Supported distros package_name yes Name used in the built package maintainer yes Name <email> string homepage yes Project URL description yes Short package description build_dependencies no List of distro package names needed to build runtime_dependencies no List of distro package names needed at runtime before_build_script no Path to a shell script run before the build rpm.spec_template no Path to a .spec.liquid override (RPM only) deb.debian_templates no Path to a directory of debian/*.liquid overrides (DEB only) (custom fields) no Arbitrary key/value pairs passed into template context

docs.title: image_caches
docs.text: Each build target pulls a container image. Image caches let you push prepared images to a registry (or save them locally) so subsequent builds skip the setup step.

docs.title: registry
docs.text:
image_caches:
  - name: my-cache
    provider: registry
    image_tag: omnipackage-cache
    registry:
      url: ghcr.io
      namespace: myorg
      username: ${GHCR_USER}
      password: ${GHCR_TOKEN}

docs.title: local
docs.text:
image_caches:
  - name: local
    provider: local
    image_tag: omnipackage-cache

The cache is populated by the prime command and consumed by build / publish / release via --image-cache <name>.

docs.title: repositories
docs.text: Each entry describes one publishing target. The publish and release commands pick one by name with --repository <name>; if omitted, the first entry is used.

docs.title: Common keys
docs.text: Key Required Description name yes Human-readable identifier; passed to --repository provider yes s3 or localfs gpg_private_key_base64 yes Base64-wrapped ASCII-armored private key; normally ${GPG_KEY} package_name no Overrides the package name used in the generated install page

docs.title: Provider: localfs
docs.text:
Writes the repository tree to a host directory. Useful for testing.

- name: Local test
  provider: localfs
  localfs:
    path: /tmp/omnipackage-repos
  gpg_private_key_base64: "${GPG_KEY}"

docs.title: Provider: s3
docs.text:
Uploads to an S3 bucket (any S3-compatible storage).

Key Required Description bucket yes Bucket name endpoint yes Full S3 endpoint URL access_key_id yes Usually ${...} from env secret_access_key yes Usually ${...} from env region no Region string; required by some providers path_in_bucket no Subdirectory prefix inside the bucket bucket_public_url no Public URL base used in the generated install page force_path_style no Default false; set true for MinIO, some R2/B2 setups cloudflare_zone_id no If set, purge this CF zone after upload cloudflare_api_token no Token used for the CF purge

See the Publishing to S3 guide for an end-to-end walkthrough.

docs.title: secrets
docs.text:
secrets: is a map of name-to-value pairs exposed to the build container as environment variables. Values typically come from ${...} substitution so the literal secret never lives in config.yml.

secrets:
  API_TOKEN: ${API_TOKEN}
  SIGNING_PASSPHRASE: ${SIGNING_PASSPHRASE}

Secrets are visible inside before_build_script and during the build itself.

docs.title: version_extractors
docs.text: A version extractor decides what version string gets stamped into the built package. At least one is required. The release and build commands pick one by name via --version-extractor <name>.

docs.title: file
docs.text:
Reads a file and applies a regex to extract the version.

version_extractors:
  - name: version_file
    provider: file
    file:
      file: VERSION
      regex: '^(.+)$'

docs.title: shell
docs.text:
Runs a shell command; stdout (trimmed) becomes the version.

version_extractors:
  - name: git_tag
    provider: shell
    shell:
      command: "git describe --tags --abbrev=0"

docs.title: constant
docs.text:
Hardcoded version string.

version_extractors:
  - name: fixed
    provider: constant
    constant:
      version: "1.0.0"

docs.title: CI/CD integration
docs.text: Running omnipackage release from CI so every tag (or every push to a release branch) produces signed packages in your repository.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• GitHub Actions workflow template
  • GitLab CI template
  • Storing the GPG key and S3 credentials as CI secrets
  • Passing secrets through to omnipackage without writing them to disk
  • Using the prime command to cache container images between runs
  • Gating releases on version extractors (e.g., only release when the tag matches)
  • Caching --build-dir between runs

docs.title: How it works
docs.text: High-level walkthrough of what happens when you run omnipackage release. This page is conceptual — it explains the model, not the flags.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• The scaffold → build → sign → publish pipeline
  • Why builds run inside containers (one per target distro)
  • How .omnipackage/config.yml maps to build jobs
  • How Liquid templates render into .spec (RPM) and debian/ (DEB) files
  • Where packages and repository metadata end up
  • How users install from the resulting repository (apt / dnf / zypper)

docs.title: Publishing to S3
docs.text: End-to-end walkthrough for turning an S3 bucket (or any S3-compatible storage) into a public DEB/RPM repository.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Bucket setup (public read, static hosting)
  • Minimal repositories: config block for S3
  • Credentials: access_key_id, secret_access_key, region, endpoint
  • Custom endpoints: Backblaze B2, Cloudflare R2, MinIO, etc. (force_path_style)
  • Optional Cloudflare cache purge (cloudflare_zone_id, cloudflare_api_token)
  • bucket_public_url for building install-page links
  • Running omnipackage release . --repository <name> to target the S3 entry
  • Verifying the generated install.html

docs.title: Signing packages
docs.text: How OmniPackage signs packages and repository metadata with GPG, and how to manage keys across environments.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Why signing matters for DEB and RPM repositories
  • Generating a key with omnipackage gpg generate
  • Using an existing GPG key (converting with omnipackage gpg convert)
  • Key formats: ASCII armor vs. base64-wrapped armor
  • Passing the key through .env / ${GPG_KEY} substitution in config.yml
  • What gets signed (DEB Release / InRelease, RPM packages and repo metadata)
  • Rotating keys

docs.title: Templates
docs.text: OmniPackage renders .spec files (RPM) and debian/ control files (DEB) from Liquid templates. This page explains what variables are available and how to customize per-distro output.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Where templates live: rpm.spec_template and deb.debian_templates in config.yml
  • Built-in variables (package name, version, maintainer, description, build/runtime deps)
  • Passing arbitrary fields through config.yml to the template context
  • Per-distro overrides via YAML anchors
  • When to write a template vs. rely on the default
  • Debugging a template (running portal into a container)
